summary: Provision a brand-new OpenSearch search/log-analytics domain. No OpenSearch estate exists yet, so this is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new OpenSearch domain
operations.summary: Request a brand-new OpenSearch domain inside the VPC; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a search/log-analytics domain as a complete specification: engine version, instance count and size, storage, network placement, and the estate's encryption baseline. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: domain_name
params.label: Domain name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, 3-28 characters. Names the domain's endpoint.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: The current recommended OpenSearch version is the default; pick engineer-decides for a legacy Elasticsearch compatibility mode.

params.name: instance_type
params.label: Data node instance type
params.help: Size class for processor and memory on every data node.

params.name: instance_count
params.label: Data node count
params.help: Two or more spread across zones keeps the domain up through a single node loss.

params.name: zone_awareness_enabled
params.label: Zone awareness
params.help: Spreads data nodes across Availability Zones. Requires an even node count.

params.name: ebs_enabled
params.label: EBS-backed storage
params.help: Always on — this baseline only offers EBS-backed domains, never instance store.

params.name: volume_size
params.label: Volume size per node (GiB)
params.help: EBS volume size attached to each data node.

params.name: encrypt_at_rest
params.label: Encryption at rest
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: node_to_node_encryption
params.label: Node-to-node encryption
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: enforce_https
params.label: Enforce HTTPS
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: tls_security_policy
params.label: Minimum TLS policy
params.help: Which TLS versions and ciphers clients may use. The 2023 forward-secrecy policy is the current recommended default.

params.name: access_policies
params.label: Access policy
params.help: The IAM resource-policy JSON controlling who can call this domain — an engineer fills this in from the estate's standard domain-access policy during review.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where the domain's data nodes live. Pick as many subnets as Availability Zones the domain spans.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the domain's endpoint.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Domain name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this domain serves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this domain.

decisions: Fine-grained access control (advanced_security_options) and its master user credentials are engineer decisions
Dedicated master nodes and UltraWarm/cold-storage tiers for larger domains are engineer decisions
Snapshot, auto-tune, and off-peak maintenance window schedules are engineer follow-ups after creation
The access policy JSON is filled in by the engineer from the estate's standard domain-access policy
